porter: benchlocal-cli v0.9.0 — single-scoreboard architecture (1 scenario per pack; per-exercise breakdown in verifier_trace.upstream_per_exercise)

description: 30-exercise lite slice across C++/Go/Java/JS/Python/Rust + edit-format adherence. Aider drives the entire batch as one subprocess; benchlocal-cli surfaces aggregate pass_rate + per-exercise breakdown. See vendor/AiderPolyglot-30/exercises.json for the curated list.

messages.content: [aider-polyglot-30] sandbox-driven batch — runner does not call this prompt directly; aider's benchmark.py drives the model loop per-exercise. This message exists only to satisfy the runner's `messages` requirement.

asserts.reason: Aider Polyglot lite uses upstream Aider's benchmark.py; per-exercise pass/fail comes from the test runs, aggregated to pass_rate by the sandbox.
